argument_substitution.mechanism: Cascade Workflows are plain Markdown prompt templates; no `{{args}}` or equivalent placeholder syntax is documented for user-provided arguments

builtin_commands.mechanism: Cascade exposes its own built-in chat slash commands (e.g. for conversation control) independently of user-authored Workflows; the `/<workflow-name>` namespace is reserved for user files

providerExtensions.name: 12,000 character per-file limit
providerExtensions.summary: Each workflow markdown file is capped at 12,000 characters; content beyond the cap fails to load or save.

providerExtensions.name: Manual-only slash-command invocation
providerExtensions.summary: Workflows never auto-invoke from model decisions or file patterns; the user must explicitly type /<workflow-name> in the Cascade input to run one.

providerExtensions.name: Workspace and global scope loading
providerExtensions.summary: Workflows load from two scopes: workspace (.windsurf/workflows/) for team-shared commands committed with the repo, and global (~/.codeium/windsurf/global_workflows/) for per-user commands available everywhere.

providerExtensions.name: Filename drives slash-command identifier
providerExtensions.summary: The markdown file basename (minus .md) is the invocation name; no frontmatter or manifest is required to register a workflow.
